questions.subject: Environment
questions.prompt:
Consider the following statements with reference to India's standing in the Global Forest Resources Assessment (GFRA) 2025:
1. India ranks among the top ten countries globally in terms of total forest area.
2. India accounts for more than 5 percent of the total global forest area.
3. India ranks fifth globally in terms of rubber plantations.

How many of the above statements are correct?
contextLines: 1. India ranks among the top ten countries globally in terms of total forest area.
2. India accounts for more than 5 percent of the total global forest area.
3. India ranks fifth globally in terms of rubber plantations.
questions.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: India has moved up one rank to the 9th position globally in terms of total forest area. Statement 2 is incorrect: India accounts for 2% of the global forest area, not more than 5%. Statement 3 is correct: India ranks 5th globally in terms of rubber plantations.
questions.takeaway: India ranks 9th globally in total forest area (accounting for 2% of the global total) and 5th in rubber plantations according to the GFRA 2025.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: India's Forest Extent: Moved up one rank to 9th position in terms of total forest area globally, accounting for 2% of global forest area; Ranks 5th in terms of rubber plantation.

questions.subject: Environment
questions.prompt:
Consider the following statements regarding the global distribution of forests as per the Global Forest Resources Assessment (GFRA) 2025:
1. Forests cover more than half of the global land area.
2. Nearly half of the world's forests are located in the tropics.
3. Europe accounts for the largest share of the world's total forest area.

How many of the above statements are correct?
contextLines: 1. Forests cover more than half of the global land area.
2. Nearly half of the world's forests are located in the tropics.
3. Europe accounts for the largest share of the world's total forest area.
questions.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect: Forests cover 32% of the global land area, which is roughly one-third, not more than half. Statement 2 is correct: Nearly half of the world's forests are located in the tropics. Statement 3 is correct: Europe has the largest forest area, accounting for 25% of the world's total.
questions.takeaway: Forests cover 32% of the global land area, with nearly half located in the tropics. Europe possesses the largest forest area globally (25%).
questions.evidenceExcerpt: Key highlights: Forests cover 32% of global land area. Nearly half of the world's forests are located in tropics... Europe has the largest forest area, accounting for 25% of world's total.

questions.subject: Environment
questions.prompt:
With reference to global forest monitoring and assessment frameworks, consider the following statements:
1. The Global Forest Resources Assessment (GFRA) is a biennial publication of the United Nations Environment Programme (UNEP).
2. The Global Forest Observations Initiative (GFOI) is a flagship programme of the Group on Earth Observations (GEO).
3. India is a member of the Group on Earth Observations (GEO).

How many of the above statements are correct?
questions.explanation:
Statement 1 is incorrect: The Global Forest Resources Assessment (GFRA) is released by the Food and Agriculture Organization (FAO) every five years, not biennially by UNEP.
Statement 2 is correct: The Global Forest Observations Initiative (GFOI) is a flagship programme of the Group on Earth Observations (GEO).
Statement 3 is correct: India is a member of the Group on Earth Observations (GEO).
questions.takeaway: The GFRA is a quinquennial (every 5 years) assessment published by the FAO. The GFOI, under which the recent plenary was held, is driven by the GEO, an intergovernmental partnership of which India is a member.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: Global Forest Resources Assessment (GFRA) 2025 Released by: FAO, every 5 years. This year's edition published during Global Forest Observations Initiative (GFOI) Plenary in Bali, Indonesia. GFOI is a flagship programme of the Group on Earth Observations (GEO) (India is a member).
